pgnViewer.reason: The source redirects to an external login page and later absorbs credentials from URL query fields.

chessMainOverseas.reason: The relative login path is observed, but the production upstream base exists only in untracked environment configuration.

contractAudit.reason: The only observed payload duplicates account and digest fields and requires fixed app and open-identity literals. The fixed open identity is not account-derived and is explicitly forbidden.
Hexadecimal MD5 is observed, but the call cannot be reproduced without the prohibited request transport and incomplete response contract.
A global browser interceptor derives Digest, Authorization, and x-date headers from browser-shipped shared signing material. The value is intentionally omitted from this report.
One source checks only content truthiness and selected message text; the other exposes a different generic management envelope. No exhaustive login response schema is established.
Selected token and uid properties are assumed, while identity responses are stored wholesale without exhaustive schemas. The second source reaches identity through a forbidden server wrapper.

One source places the account token in selected POST bodies while applying shared signing and cookies globally; the other indiscriminately adds a Bearer header to its management client. Neither proves the required endpoint-scoped direct-browser format.
Client storage durations are observed, but they are not tied to a verified server token expiry. Restoration lacks strict schemas and server revalidation.
Both sources reject generic failures without proving global authentication loss for 401 or permission-only handling for 403.
Only partial local deletion and external redirection are observed. No remote logout contract or complete private-state cleanup lifecycle is established.

decisiveStopReasons: The only direct password-login implementation requires browser-shipped shared signing material.
The only direct password-login payload includes a fixed incompatible open identity.
The exact production upstream base is unavailable in tracked source.
Response, identity, expiry, restoration, 401, 403, and logout contracts are incomplete or conflicting.
actionsDeliberatelyNotTaken: No credential form or password transformation was added to the target.
No token persistence adapter or protected route was added.
No account credentials or sensitive literal values were read into this report.
minimumRequiredExternalEvidence: A tracked exact HTTPS production base with a browser deployment contract.
A password-login request that requires neither shared browser signing material nor a fixed open identity.
Exhaustive login, token, identity, and error response schemas.
Server-defined token expiry and restoration behavior.
Endpoint-scoped authenticated request formatting that keeps public reads credential-free.
Explicit 401 authentication-loss, 403 permission, and logout contracts.
